description: Students explore an amusement park map and learn French vocabulary for attractions and treats by matching rides and snacks to their labels.
Building on amusement park vocabulary, students use a video (« Mini Météo Été : Manège ») and listening strategies to learn more about visiting a park.
Students compare ways of sending correspondence and learn the conventions and vocabulary for writing emails in French.
Students explore text-message conversations, comparing texting with phone calls and learning the informal French language used in texts.

french_title: Les conversations téléphoniques et les messages
description: Through the video « L'inconnu du téléphone », students learn vocabulary and conventions for French phone conversations and leaving messages.
Centered on Earth Day (April 22), students explore the importance of protecting the environment and learn related French vocabulary.
Students learn French sports vocabulary while discovering sports that originate from Indigenous peoples in Canada through a video.

french_title: Le cinéma et les critiques de film
description: Students explore the history of cinema via the video « Top 5 sur le cinéma » and learn French vocabulary for talking about and reviewing films.
Using the video « Trouve l'objet : le verre d'eau au restaurant », students learn restaurant vocabulary and how to order and communicate with a server in French.
Students identify different types of housing and learn French vocabulary for homes and their various rooms and parts.
Students explore leisure activities through an audio street-interview (micro-trottoir) clip and learn related French vocabulary.
Students learn French idiomatic expressions that carry a second, figurative meaning, such as « raconter des salades ».
Students explore the concept of parties and celebrations and learn the French vocabulary used to describe different kinds of fêtes.
Through a video about a Norouz celebration, students learn how to understand and create party invitations in French.
Using the text « Des frissons avant le match », students examine a dialogue about nervousness and learn to express emotions in French.
Students learn to express varied daily emotions in French and explore relaxation techniques through a matching game and activities.
Students explore leisure activities and beaches of the Caribbean islands while learning related French vocabulary.
Students learn the parts of a postcard, matching images with personal messages and practicing how to write postcards in French.
Students explore different types of clubs and the benefits of joining them, learning related French vocabulary.
Through a video about a school club in Thunder Bay, students discuss clubs available to them and answer questions in French.
Students explore a video about healthy snacks and learn French vocabulary related to food and snacking.
Using a healthy-eating plate diagram, students learn to make healthy food choices and the French vocabulary for fruits, vegetables, and balanced meals.
Students learn that French is spoken across Canada and beyond Quebec, exploring francophone communities in the Americas.
Students examine travel posters and their typical format (country name, image, slogan) and learn to create their own in French.
